entries.description: SRA (AAAI 2026) is the primary concurrent work: supervises BERT softmax attention with KL divergence loss against HateXplain rationale masks, claims 2.4x better explainability than baselines. Uses softmax — produces no exactly-zero attention weights.
entries.notes: Establishes that rationale-supervised attention for hate speech is viable; our sparsemax contribution is structurally distinct

entries.description: HateXplain annotates 20K posts with rationale spans: avg 5.47 of 23.42 tokens highlighted (~25% density). The low rationale density is structurally compatible with sparse attention — most tokens should have zero weight.
entries.notes: Key dataset property: ~25% token density means sparsemax's structural sparsity is well-matched

entries.description: Sparsemax (Martins & Astudillo, ICML 2016) is differentiable, produces exact-zero probabilities for low-scoring items, and has an efficient Jacobian supporting backpropagation through constrained projection.
entries.notes: Confirms technical feasibility of using sparsemax as drop-in for softmax in attention heads

entries.description: α-entmax (Correia et al., EMNLP 2019) enables learnable sparsity per head with no accuracy cost on MT. Heads specialize: some dense (α≈1), some focused (α≈2). α=2 recovers sparsemax.
entries.notes: Design option: fix α=2 (sparsemax) or learn α; rationale supervision may drive heads toward α≈2

entries.description: The 'attention is explanation' debate (Jain & Wallace 2019 vs. Wiegreffe & Pinter 2019) is unresolved for softmax attention. Sparsemax sidesteps this debate: zero-weight tokens are provably excluded from the representation, making faithfulness a structural property rather than a probabilistic one.
entries.notes: Our contribution reframes the debate: supervised sparsemax makes faithfulness verifiable, not just arguable

entries.description: ERASER standardizes comprehensiveness (AOPC) and sufficiency (AOPC) metrics. Comprehensiveness measures prediction change when rationale is deleted; sufficiency measures whether rationale alone is sufficient for prediction. Mini-project found significant comprehensiveness improvement with sparsemax.
entries.notes: Standard evaluation framework; comprehensiveness improvement is the strongest preliminary signal from mini-project

entries.description: No paper combines sparsemax (or α-entmax) with supervised human rationale alignment in any classification task. All rationale-supervised attention work (SRA, IvRA, HuMAL, Bouchacourt & Baroni, regularization2023attention) uses softmax attention.
entries.notes: PRIMARY GAP: confirmed by exhaustive search across 54 papers; this is Gap 1 from research-landscape.md

entries.description: Whether hard sparsity constraint (exactly-zero attention on non-rationale tokens) degrades classification is unknown. 'Learning from Sufficient Rationales' (2025) shows rationale informativeness for sufficiency is complex — the relationship between annotation quality and model sufficiency is nonlinear.
entries.notes: Gap 2: faithfulness-performance trade-off under hard sparsity; explains inconsistent mini-project sufficiency

entries.description: Cross-dataset generalization of rationale-supervised models is untested. HateCOT addresses generalization via LLM chain-of-thought; KDD 2024 identifies target-aware generalizable fairness as open. No sparsemax rationale model has been evaluated cross-dataset.
entries.notes: Gap 3: deployment-critical but secondary to Gaps 1 and 2

entries.description: RISE (IJCNN 2025) uses multi-task learning with BIO rationale tagging as auxiliary task; outperforms SOTA on HateXplain. Different approach from attention supervision — does not modify attention mechanism.
entries.notes: Adjacent competitor using MTL; demonstrates that rationale guidance in HSD is active research area in 2025

entries.description: Mini-project (3 seeds, BERT-base, HateXplain) found significant comprehensiveness gains with sparsemax over softmax baseline using ERASER metrics. Sufficiency was inconsistent across seeds.
entries.notes: PRELIMINARY ONLY: needs replication with proper 5-seed design; motivates the full research project
